message:
chore: sync beads issue tracking state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -205,7 +205,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.3","title":"T6.7.7.3 Concurrent-writer fairness and durability suite with critical-section timing logs","description":"Own the proof bundle for the pager de-serialization work: deterministic concurrent e2e workloads, fairness measurements, checkpoint/durability coverage, and structured logs that expose where time is still spent inside unavoidable critical sections.","acceptance_criteria":"1. End-to-end multi-writer scenarios cover throughput, fairness, checkpoint interaction, and durability on file-backed storage.
2. Structured logs record critical-section timing, queueing/latency symptoms, batch identifiers where relevant, and first-failure diagnostics.
3. Replayable evidence demonstrates that the refactor preserved correctness while reducing time spent in the old choke points.
4. The proof pack is usable as the regression guard for future pager changes.","notes":"Verification blueprint:
- Unit/property layer:
  - Cover pager invariants after lock-surface reduction, checkpoint safety, write-staging correctness, and preservation of concurrent-mode default behavior.
- E2E/replay layer:
  - Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_pager_concurrency.sh` for multi-writer throughput/fairness workloads, checkpoint overlap, restart/durability replay, and lock-contention regression checks.
  - Include deterministic seeds for conflict-heavy and conflict-light workloads.
- Logging contract:
  - Emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `critical_section_name`, `hold_ns`, `wait_ns`, `writer_count`, `checkpoint_overlap`, `backend_identity`, and first-failure diagnostics.
- User-facing intent:
  - Multi-writer users should see both better fairness and clearer evidence about where serialization still remains.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:06.306194278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T04:20:15.195341408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","mvcc","optimization","pager","performance","storage-wiring","track6","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:13.572478467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:06.306194278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:13.212942267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4","title":"T6.7.7.4 MVCC-native snapshot publication plane for pager metadata and page visibility","description":"Introduce an MVCC-native snapshot publication plane for pager metadata and page-visibility state. Instead of forcing steady-state readers and write staging through shared ownership of coarse pager state, publish reader-consumable snapshots or generation-stamped metadata views using an evidence-backed mechanism such as partitioned ownership, epoch/RCU-style publication, seqlock-style publication, or an equivalent design.

This is the architectural jump from "less locking" to "structurally cheap reads and visibility checks." The exact primitive is not pre-selected, but the outcome is: readers should bind to a coherent published view of relevant pager state without paying hidden global-ownership tax.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Steady-state readers can consult a published pager/visibility snapshot or equivalent metadata view without taking the old global ownership path for ordinary access.
2. Publication invariants are explicit for commit visibility, page-state versioning, checkpoint boundaries, and stale-reader retry behavior.
3. Deterministic unit/property tests cover monotonic publication, stale/superseded reader views, retry behavior, ABA-like hazards, and reclamation safety when applicable.
4. Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_snapshot_publication.sh` to exercise file-backed concurrent-reader/concurrent-writer scenarios with fixed seeds, deterministic schedules where available, and fallback rejection enabled.
5. Structured logs emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `snapshot_gen`, `visible_commit_seq`, `publication_mode`, `read_retry_count`, `page_set_size`, `elapsed_ns`, and first-failure diagnostics.","notes":"Future-self intent:
- This bead is deliberately frontier-leaning. It is the place to use serious publication techniques if the evidence supports them.
- Do not reduce it to a thinner mutex wrapper; the whole point is a genuinely published metadata plane that makes MVCC visible in the physical runtime structure.
- User-facing payoff: lower read inflation during write pressure, better fairness, and a storage story that is truly differentiated from SQLite's serialized guts.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":420,"created_at":"2026-03-06T06:45:57.056859619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:51:38.952293064Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-uplift","architecture-audit-2026-03","mvcc","next-gen-architecture","optimization","pager","performance","promised-land","storage-wiring","track6","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:45:57.056859619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:46:00.825820298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8","title":"T6.7.8 Make WAL page indexing authoritative and bound refresh/read cost","description":"`fsqlite-wal` still pays too much lookup and refresh tax because the WAL index is not yet authoritative enough for the steady-state path. Reverse scans, capped indexing behavior, and refresh-oriented compensation logic make the runtime pay repeatedly for uncertainty that should instead be resolved once in the write/publication path.

This bead is therefore more than a localized WAL micro-optimization. It is the move that turns WAL metadata into a first-class part of the concurrency architecture: authoritative, incrementally maintained, and cheap for readers and commit coordination to consult without rediscovering state on the hot path.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The steady-state WAL read path no longer depends on reverse scans or broad refresh work to resolve page/frame visibility under normal operation.
2. WAL metadata becomes authoritative enough that readers and commit/checkpoint coordination can consult it directly with bounded lookup cost and explicit invariants.
3. Deterministic tests and failure-injection suites cover truncation, torn tail, corruption, recovery, and incremental refresh behavior without semantic drift.
4. Benchmarks from T6.7.1 show reduced WAL lookup/refresh tax and clearer attribution of remaining write-path cost.","notes":"Execution split:
- `bd-1dp9.6.7.8.1`: generation/index invariants and authoritative lookup contract.
- `bd-1dp9.6.7.8.4`: commit-published WAL visibility plane.
- `bd-1dp9.6.7.8.2`: incremental refresh / steady-state lookup cutover.
- `bd-1dp9.6.7.8.3`: corruption/truncate/torn-tail evidence pack.

Planning intent:
- Treat this as concurrency-substrate work, not just a read-side cleanup task.
- The long-term goal is that WAL metadata publication becomes something the rest of the engine can trust structurally, rather than revalidating through hot-path scans.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":600,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:02:05.040972610Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:46:04.632424916Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","optimization","performance","storage-wiring","track6","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:02:05.040972610Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.1","title":"T6.7.8.1 WAL generation/index invariants and authoritative lookup contract","description":"Define and instrument the correctness contract for an authoritative WAL page index per WAL generation. This subtask makes reset/truncate/ABA/torn-tail behavior explicit so the optimization does not blur exceptional recovery conditions into the steady-state fast path.","acceptance_criteria":"1. WAL generation/index invariants are explicit for steady-state, reset/truncate, ABA, torn-tail, and corruption/recovery cases.
2. Instrumentation and deterministic tests validate generation identity, invalidation, and lookup-contract behavior.
3. The contract distinguishes normal authoritative-index lookup from exceptional fallback/recovery handling.
4. Future implementers can reason about the correctness envelope without re-deriving WAL semantics from code archaeology.","notes":"Planning intent:
- This is a support slice for `bd-1dp9.6.7.8.4`, not the destination by itself.
- The invariants exist so WAL truth can be published structurally in the commit path without smearing recovery edge cases across the steady-state fast path.
- Do not stop at "good enough lookup diagnostics"; the real target is authoritative, publishable metadata.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:07.018609059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:47:38.342741142Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","optimization","performance","storage-wiring","track6","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:13.930234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:07.018609059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.1","title":"T6.7.8.1 WAL generation/index invariants and authoritative lookup contract","description":"Define and instrument the correctness contract for an authoritative WAL page index per WAL generation. This subtask makes reset/truncate/ABA/torn-tail behavior explicit so the optimization does not blur exceptional recovery conditions into the steady-state fast path.","acceptance_criteria":"1. WAL generation/index invariants are explicit for steady-state, reset/truncate, ABA, torn-tail, and corruption/recovery cases.
2. Instrumentation and deterministic tests validate generation identity, invalidation, and lookup-contract behavior.
3. The contract distinguishes normal authoritative-index lookup from exceptional fallback/recovery handling.
4. Future implementers can reason about the correctness envelope without re-deriving WAL semantics from code archaeology.","notes":"AmberOx (Codex): claiming task and beginning implementation on WAL generation/index invariants + instrumentation + deterministic tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:07.018609059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T07:56:13.377173792Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","optimization","performance","storage-wiring","track6","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:13.930234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:07.018609059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.2","title":"T6.7.8.2 Incremental refresh and steady-state lookup cutover off reverse scans","description":"Implement the authoritative WAL index and incremental refresh path so normal reads no longer degrade into reverse frame scans and refresh cost scales with newly appended frames. The design must preserve correct handling of generation changes and damaged tails.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Steady-state latest-page lookup uses the authoritative index for the current WAL generation.
2. Refresh work scales with appended frames and generation changes instead of repeated full-log scan/parse behavior.
3. Deterministic tests cover append/refresh concurrency, generation resets, and correctness under edge conditions defined in the invariants bead.
4. Structured logs expose lookup mode, refresh deltas, generation changes, and fallback/recovery reasons.","notes":"User-facing payoff:
- Read latency and read-amid-write behavior become more predictable because normal WAL lookup stops paying hidden scan tax.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:07.743787496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:51:40.554439082Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","optimization","performance","storage-wiring","track6","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:07.743787496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:46:02.722848577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.3","title":"T6.7.8.3 WAL truncate/torn-tail/corruption suite with authoritative-index trace pack","description":"Build the proof bundle for authoritative WAL indexing: deterministic edge-case tests, corruption/truncate/torn-tail scenarios, and structured logs proving the fast path is used only when generation semantics are sound.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic tests cover truncate/reset, torn tails, corruption/recovery entry, and concurrent append/refresh scenarios.
2. Structured logs expose generation identity, lookup mode, recovery/fallback reasons, and first-failure diagnostics.
3. Replay instructions and artifact hashes are published and tied back to the T6.7.1 artifact contract.
4. The evidence pack demonstrates reduced lookup/refresh cost in steady state without hiding exceptional cases.","notes":"Verification blueprint:
- Unit/property layer:
  - Cover WAL generation identity, authoritative index invalidation, append/refresh concurrency, truncate/reset, torn tails, and corruption/recovery entry conditions.
- E2E/replay layer:
  - Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_wal_index.sh` for steady-state lookup, write-heavy refresh, truncate/reset, torn-tail simulation, and corruption-triggered fallback behavior.
- Logging contract:
  - Emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `wal_generation`, `lookup_mode`, `refresh_delta_frames`, `fallback_reason`, `recovery_mode`, `elapsed_ns`, and first-failure diagnostics.
- User-facing intent:
  - Read-path predictability should improve, while exceptional WAL states remain explicit instead of silently blended into the hot path.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:08.462162169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T04:20:15.557025986Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","optimization","performance","storage-wiring","track6","verification","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:15.012148227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:08.462162169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:14.649117546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.4","title":"T6.7.8.4 Commit-published WAL visibility map and generation snapshots","description":"Move WAL metadata publication into the commit path so readers and commit/checkpoint coordination can bind to a commit-published visibility map plus generation snapshot rather than rediscovering the same truth through scans and refresh work. Concretely, writers should publish enough authoritative latest-frame metadata per generation that steady-state readers can perform bounded lookup against a coherent snapshot.

This is the WAL-side counterpart to the pager publication bead: not just faster lookup, but a structural change in where truth is established and how the rest of the engine consumes it.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Commit publication incrementally maintains an authoritative WAL visibility structure for the active generation, sufficient for bounded latest-frame lookup in steady-state reads.
2. Readers bind to a generation-stamped metadata snapshot and do not rely on reverse scans for ordinary visibility resolution.
3. Deterministic unit/property tests cover generation rollover, truncate/reset, torn tail, corruption, recovery, and snapshot coherence under append pressure.
4. Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_wal_publication_plane.sh` to run file-backed append/read/checkpoint scenarios with fixed seeds and validate both semantics and metadata-publication behavior.
5. Structured logs emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `wal_generation`, `publication_seq`, `frame_delta_count`, `latest_frame_entries`, `snapshot_age`, `lookup_mode`, `fallback_reason`, and first-failure diagnostics.","notes":"Future-self intent:
- Treat this as publication architecture, not a lookup-table convenience feature.
- The win is not only speed; it is that the rest of the runtime can trust WAL truth as a published substrate instead of repeatedly reconstructing it.
- User-facing payoff: more predictable read-amid-write behavior, lower WAL-induced variance, and cleaner diagnosis when recovery modes are actually invoked.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-03-06T06:45:57.818991776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:47:40.274959916Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-uplift","architecture-audit-2026-03","next-gen-architecture","optimization","performance","promised-land","storage-wiring","track6","verification","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:46:02.344146975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:45:57.818991776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:46:01.965321181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
